adult.dataset_description: UCI Census Income dataset. Predicts whether annual income exceeds $50K based on demographic features. Contains 8 nominal categorical columns (workclass, education, marital_status, occupation, relationship, race, sex, native_country) with no natural ordering except education (but education_num already encodes this). No true ID columns.
must_do: encode_categorical with one_hot for nominal columns
must_not_do: Do NOT use ordinal encoding on nominal categories
adult.notes: Dataset domain: demographics/census. Categorical nature: mostly nominal (no natural ordering). High-cardinality columns: native_country (41 values), occupation (14), education (16). ML model encoding guidance: One-hot encoding is strongly preferred. KNN uses Euclidean distance — ordinal encoding of nominal features makes distance meaningless (distance between 'race=1' and 'race=3' would be 2, implying they're more different than 'race=1' and 'race=2'). One-hot preserves equal distances between categories.
